weather_evidence_attempt.conclusion: Neither the outdoor kickoff-hour weather path nor an official_roof confirmation could be validated in this run. This is recorded for the audit trail per the task's explicit instruction to still attempt weather/roof evidence collection even when the Elo path has already failed closed; it is a secondary, independent fail-closed condition (weather_evidence_missing_or_invalid), not the reason predictions were withheld (Elo capture failure alone already blocks the entire path).

diagnosis: This isolated run's shell sandbox reaches the internet through an allowlisted egress proxy. Both https://www.eloratings.net and https://api.weather.gov are not on that allowlist as of this run (confirmed non-transient across two Elo attempts ~25s apart; a control request to https://example.com was also proxy-blocked with the identical X-Proxy-Error: blocked-by-allowlist header, indicating a restrictive default allowlist rather than a site-specific outage). This is a recurrence of the identical condition observed in the 2026-07-15 07:05Z daily run (see memory: 'Elo capture 403 blocked-by-allowlist AGAIN 7/15 07:05Z') and in the 2026-07-14 16:05Z sf101 finalization run. capture_elo_evidence.py could not perform its mandated direct_http_response_body capture (urlopen against OFFICIAL_ELO_SOURCE with HTTP 200 + exact final-URL check). No TSV or receipt file was created by either Elo attempt. The workspace web_fetch tool, used only as a diagnostic/audit-trail attempt on the weather path (per this task's own instructions), also produced no usable content for the api.weather.gov endpoint.
explicitly_not_done: Did NOT fetch World.tsv content via any alternate tool (e.g. general-purpose web fetch) and write it into an evidence file, because that would fabricate a capture_method=direct_http_response_body receipt for bytes that were not actually captured by the sanctioned direct-HTTP path.

Did NOT proceed to context template / market population / validate_context / run_tests / predict_jul11.py finalize steps, since the fail-closed contract requires stopping the entire official prediction path on Elo capture failure.
Did NOT substitute estimated Elo ratings or previously known cached values as a stand-in for a fresh capture.
Did NOT assert weather_decision=indoor_no_weather without a retained official_roof source explicitly confirming roof_status=closed for this fixture; no such source was obtained.
Did NOT create or overwrite any evidence/sf102_*.final.json artifact.
recommended_action: Fix the egress proxy allowlist to permit www.eloratings.net and api.weather.gov before 2026-07-15T19:00:00Z kickoff, then retry both the Elo capture and the weather/roof evidence capture from an environment with confirmed network access (or from the user's local machine). Approximately 2h53m of runway remained at the time of this report (16:07 UTC). If no successful capture occurs before kickoff, sf102 has no official artifact and must be recorded as a missed finalization, consistent with the sf101 (2026-07-14) and July 11 incident precedents in AUTOMATION_RUNBOOK.md. Both semifinals will then lack official pre-kickoff artifacts, which also means the w-grid refit gate (currently pending after both SFs are graded) will have no live_current_elo rows contributed by either finalization run - grading must instead rely on final scorelines and whatever market/context evidence is separately reconstructable post-match.
